items.title: Gurriers at The Button Factory, Dublin
items.summary: The post Gurriers at The Button Factory, Dublin appeared first on Goldenplec.com Gurriers at The Button Factory, Dublin on Friday December 19th, 2026 Gurriers, the last diamond to be forged from Ireland’s post-punk scene before the mine collapsed, played two sold-out shows in The Button Factory this week on a landmark occasion for a group who have outgrown their sweaty club origins.

items.title: Kingfishr at 3Arena, Dublin
items.summary: The post Kingfishr at 3Arena, Dublin appeared first on Goldenplec.com   Kingfishr at 3Arena, Dublin on December 18th 2025 One of the most anticipated concerts of the year occurred on an absolutely freezing Thursday night, with Kingfishr taking the stage of the 3arena for the first of their two sold out shows.

items.title: Alexi Cory-Smith, Bella Figura Co-Founder and CEO, Dies at 58
items.summary: The firm, which acquires and manages rights across music publishing and recordings, announced Cory-Smith's unexpected passing on Friday (July 3).

items.title: Michael Stipe & Brandi Carlile Cover Elvis Presley, Josh Homme Covers Tom Petty At Gansett Games
items.summary: The Gansett Games is an annual field day in Amagansett, NY hosted by billionaire crypto investor Mike Novogratz. The event was shouted out in T-shirt form on an episode of Billions back in 2022. It seems like a premier rich-people gathering, and Novogratz rounded up a lot of rock stars for this year's closing ceremony on June 27.

items.title: Touch & Go Co-Founder Dave Stimson Has Died
items.summary: Dave Stimson, co-founder of the pioneering '80s hardcore punk zine, record label, and distribution company Touch & Go, has died. Stimson reportedly passed away Wednesday. Touch & Go's Corey Rusk posted the news to the label's Instagram account today along with a tribute to Stimson: The post Touch & Go Co-Founder Dave Stimson Has Died appeared first on Stereogum.

items.title: Julian Casablancas Expands On Zionism Comments In Oxford Union Speech
items.summary: When Julian Casablancas appeared on SubwayTakes back in April, host Kareem Rahma asked the Strokes singer for his most controversial take. Casablancas replied, "American Zionists get the benefits of white privileged people but talk like they are Black people during slavery."
